data.short_description: Coushatta Casino Resort, Louisiana's largest casino resort, will hold the grand opening of its new Legacy Tower on May 15, adding 204 guest rooms including 100 luxury suites and bringing total room inventory to over 1,000. The ceremony, hosted by the Coushatta Tribe of Louisiana, will include a tribal ceremony, ribbon cutting, and first-look tours of the new tower.

data.location: 260+ hotels across 12 European countries and 146 cities, Address not specified
data.status: filed for insolvency; approximately 125 hotels in Germany and Austria will continue to operate
data.short_description: Revo Hospitality Group, Europe's largest white-label hotel operator managing over 260 hotels across 12 countries, has filed for insolvency under self-administration at Charlottenburg District Court. The company cited increased wage costs, higher expenses for rent, energy, and food, and integration problems from rapid expansion as key factors; approximately 125 hotels in Germany and Austria will continue operating with all 5,500 employees.

data.location: UPMC Altoona, Altoona, PA, Address not specified

data.short_description: UPMC Altoona began offering Pluvicto, a radiopharmaceutical therapy for metastatic prostate cancer, starting in April 2026, becoming the first satellite location in the UPMC Hillman Cancer Center network to provide this treatment. The expansion reduces the need for Blair County patients to travel to Pittsburgh for the therapy.

The new Plantation Publix near Fort Lauderdale will open on June 4 according to the company's opening soon page.

data.location: West Gate City Blvd spot in Greensboro
data.status: abruptly shutter all of its remaining locations without warning in late April 2026
data.short_description: Smokey Bones has been a notable player in the casual-dining barbecue space but after experiencing severe financial headwinds the parent company FAT Brands pushed the chain into Chapter 11 bankruptcy. This financial collapse led the company to abruptly shutter all of its remaining locations without warning in late April 2026. Heading into June communities across the state including prominent locations like the West Gate City Blvd spot in Greensboro are left with entirely vacant buildings.

data.status: is opening the first Middlesex County Baked Bear at 11 a.m. March 8
data.short_description: The Korpal family is opening the first Middlesex County Baked Bear at 11 a.m. March 8 at 72 Easton Ave. in New Brunswick just a few blocks from the Rutgers campus. The business will offer custom ice cream cookie sandwiches where customers can choose from cookie varieties and ice cream flavors. The New Brunswick location will be the second Central Jersey store following a Westfield spot that opened last weekend.

data.status: set to permanently close on Sunday, May 30

data.location: Pittsburgh Mills Mall, Tarentum, PA, Address not specified
data.short_description: The Macy's at Pittsburgh Mills Mall in Tarentum, PA closed on April 26, 2026, marking it as the first of 14 Macy's locations announced to close in the first half of 2026. Prior to closure, the store held a clearance sale with deep discounts on shoes, clothing, and home goods, with all sales final and no returns accepted.

data.location: 86th and Cottage Grove, Chicago, IL, Address not specified (no zip)
data.short_description: Walgreens announced the closure of its longtime Chatham location at 86th and Cottage Grove on June 4, citing over $1 million in losses last year, a 30% drop in prescription volume, and theft rates far above the company average. The decision has drawn backlash from residents who say the closure will create a "prescription desert" and disproportionately impact seniors and the Black community.

data.location: Fukuyama’s Shinichi district (former Big Shinichi location)
data.short_description: Lopia the discount supermarket brand operated by OIC Group announced its second Hiroshima location will open on May 15 in Fukuyama’s Shinichi district. This expansion marks Lopia’s first entry into eastern Hiroshima. The new Lopia Shinichi store will occupy the former Big Shinichi location which closed in 2023.

data.status: will open its Southampton stand at the Marlands Shopping Centre on May 16
data.short_description: Spud Fusion which attracts around four million viewers a month on social media will open its Southampton stand at the Marlands Shopping Centre on May 16. First launched in Portsmouth Spud Fusion opened as a food van selling loaded jacket potatoes last year and is now expanding to the city. The new store will offer a seating area for customers.

data.status: will be shut to guests / closing its doors until 2027
data.short_description: Jumeirah Burj Al Arab will be shut to guests with a reopening expected late next year. The 60-storey hotel opened in 1999 is undergoing a carefully phased restoration programme designed to refresh and upgrade the hotel's iconic decor. The programme has been developed through long-term planning following more than 25 years of continuous operation.

data.store_name: Publix Super Market at Cold Spring Pointe
data.short_description: Publix supermarket at Cold Spring Pointe is opening its doors next month. Doors on the 48,387-square-foot store will open at 7 a.m. June 17. The new store located at 5401 Alexandria Pike is the second of five Publix supermarkets slated for opening in the area.

data.status: will close its doors on Saturday, May 9
data.short_description: Tender Lovin' Chix is leaving Uptown. What started as a food truck and moved into its brick-and-mortar storefront in August 2024 will close its doors on Saturday May 9. We are officially moving locations and getting ready for our next chapter.

data.status: opening of Balam Coffee and Wine on March 16
data.short_description: The siblings behind Ferndale’s Mezcal Mexican Bar and Kitchen expanded the family business on March 16 with the opening of Balam Coffee and Wine. Located three doors down from Mezcal Balam sources its coffee from Mexico and South America and has a rotating selection of red and white wines from Latin America. The menu offers hot and iced coffee Mexican pastries and food.

The Chick-fil-A expansion in Michigan continued with a new location in Royal Oak located on Woodward Avenue and 13 Mile Road. The Royal Oak location held its grand opening on March 26. As part of the grand opening both establishments donated $25,000 to local hunger relief charities.

The Chick-fil-A expansion in Michigan continued with a new location in Lincoln Park at the intersection of Southfield Road and Dix Road. The Lincoln Park location held its grand opening on April 2. As part of the grand opening both establishments donated $25,000 to local hunger relief charities.

data.status: opened its doors to the public in late March
data.short_description: Little Bella’s Pizza may be on your list of places to check out if you’re in the mood for brick-oven pizzas charcutiers and cocktails. Located at 100 S. Main St. the newest establishment from restaurateur Adam Merkel opened its doors to the public in late March. The restaurant’s pizza menu offers classic pies including pepperoni and margherita along with garlic lovers shrimp scampi and more.

data.location: 230 E. Main St., Newark Shopping Center, Newark
data.status: is opening its second Delaware location
data.short_description: Nanu's Hot Chicken in Newark will be the latest chicken fast-food joint to join Delaware's growing hot chicken scene. The Philadelphia-based franchise announced on Instagram it will open its second Delaware location in the Newark Shopping Center at 230 E. Main St.

data.location: Prairie Town Center, 215 Junction Road, Madison
data.short_description: IKEA U.S. announced the plans for the new store. The new store is scheduled to open in fall 2026 at Prairie Town Center 215 Junction Road on Madison's far-west side. The Madison store will be a 54,000 square-foot space and will offer more than 5,000 products around 3,000 of which will be available to take home immediately from the store.

data.status: will permanently close its doors this Sunday, May 3
data.short_description: A boat-to-table mainstay in Dana Point Harbor is closing for good after 46 years in business the owners announced. Jon’s Fish Market will permanently close its doors this Sunday May 3. Owner Shala Mansur O’Keefe said she made the difficult decision to shutter the family business because of a combination of personal health concerns and economic pressures.

data.location: 1600 Village Market Place, Park West Village, Morrisville
data.short_description: Shake Shack is opening another location in the Triangle. Shake Shack will bring its menu of sandwiches fries and milkshakes to the Raleigh suburb of Morrisville at the Park West Village shopping center. The Morrisville location will be open to the public starting 10 a.m. Wednesday May 13.

data.location: second San Antonio store at The Forum shopping center
data.short_description: Teso Life shared the latest on their second store opening up in the San Antonio area at The Forum shopping center. Teso Life is certainly no exception with its overwhelming catalogue of mouth-watering edibles homewares cutesy stationary and beauty and skincare supplies.

data.short_description: Lotus of Siam is back at its original home inside the Commercial Center on Sahara Avenue more than four years after closing that location. Located at 7003 North Pine Island Road/88th Avenue business hours are 4:30 a.m.–8 p.m. weekdays and 5 a.m.–8 p.m. weekends.

data.status: may be closing soon; final day could be May 15
data.short_description: Social media posts in Universal Orlando Annual Passholder groups indicate the Mystic Fountain may close on May 15, though Universal Orlando has not officially confirmed the closure. The interactive talking fountain has been a fan-favorite attraction for multiple decades.

The Way Way Store in Saco, a nearly 100-year-old antique and unique goods retailer founded in 1929, held a going-away yard sale on Saturday as owner Peter Scontras decided to retire following his wife Bridget's passing. The store was known for selling canoes, antique road signs, old cars, and penny candy after the Scontras family took over in 2011.
